verStmt.version: v1: Edited, anonymised dataset for public distribution
verStmt.notes: This version was retrieved by DataFirst on the 29 March 2022.

stdyInfo.abstract: The Quarterly Labour Force Survey (QLFS) is a household-based sample survey conducted by Statistics South Africa (Stats SA). It collects data on the labour market activities of individuals aged 15 years or older who live in South Africa.

sumDscr.geogUnit: Provincial and metropolitan level, as well as by geographic type.
sumDscr.universe: The QLFS sample covers the non-institutional population of South Africa with one exception. The only institutional subpopulation included in the QLFS sample are individuals in worker's hostels. Persons living in private dwelling units within institutions are also enumerated. For example, within a school compound, one would enumerate the schoolmaster's house and teachers' accommodation because these are private dwellings. Students living in a dormitory on the school compound would, however, be excluded.

stdyInfo.notes:
For Q4 2021 Statistics South Africa contiued to use Computer Assisted Telephone Interviewing (CATI), in an attempt to reduce the spread of COVID-19. This meant that households without telephones were out of scope. See the statistical release for more information.
INDIVIDUALS: labour market activity, labour preferences, labour market history, demographic characteristics, marital status, employment status, education, grants, tax.
From 2010 the income data collected by South Africa's Quarterly Labour Force Survey is no longer provided in the QLFS dataset (except for a brief return in QLFS 2010 Q3 which may be an error). Possibly because the data is unreliable at the level of the quarter, Statistics South Africa now provides the income data from the QLFS in an annualised dataset called Labour Market Dynamics in South Africa (LMDSA). The datasets for LMDSA are available from DataFirst's website.

dataColl.sampProc:
The QLFS uses a master sampling frame that is used by several household surveys conducted by Statistics South Africa. This wave of the QLFS is based on the 2013 master frame, which was created based on the 2011 census. There are 3324 PSUs in the master frame and roughly 33 000 dwelling units.
The sample for the QLFS is based on a stratified two-stage design with probability proportional to size (PPS) sampling of PSUs in the first stage, and sampling of dwelling units (DUs) with systematic sampling in the second stage.
For each quarter of the QLFS, a quarter of the sampled dwellings are rotated out of the sample. These dwellings are replaced by new dwellings from the same PSU or the next PSU on the list. For more information see the statistical release.
dataColl.collSitu: COVID 19 affected data collection for QLFS 2020 Q4. Please see the sampling section and the statistical release for more on this.
dataColl.weight: The sample weights were constructed in order to account for the following: the original selection probabilities (design weights); adjustments for PSUs that were sub-sampled or segmented; excluded population from the sampling frame (EAs with insufficient DUs); non-response; weight trimming; benchmarking to known population estimates from the Demographic Analysis Chief Directorate within Stats SA; and raking to bias-adjusted control estimates from a bias-adjustment procedure to compensate for the non-coverage bias in the sample due to only observing those households that can be contacted by telephone.

method.notes: In general, imputation is used for item non-response (i.e. blanks within the questionnaire) and edit failures (i.e. invalid or inconsistent responses). The eligible households in the sampled dwellings can be divided into two response categories: respondents and non-respondents. Weight adjustment is applied to account for the non-respondent households (e.g. refusal, no contact, etc.). The adjustment for total non-response was computed at two levels of non-response: PSU non-response and household non-response.

useStmt.citReq: Statistics South Africa. Quarterly Labour Force Survey 2021: Q4 [dataset]. Version 1. Pretoria: Statistics South Africa [producer], 2022. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2022. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/kc6k-g071
useStmt.conditions: Public access data for use under a Creative Commons CC-BY (Attribution-only) License

catgry.labl: Living together like husband and wife
Bachelors Degree and Post Graduate Diploma
Pre-school (including day care; crèche; Grade R and Pre-Grade R in an ECD Centre)
Ordinary School (Including Grade R Learners Who Attend a Formal School; Grade 1-12 Learners and Learners in Special Class)
Adult Basic Education and Training Centre (ABET CENTRE)

var.name: Q25APDWRK
var.labl: Have paid work to return to

var.name: Q25BOWNBUSNS
var.labl: Have own business to return to

var.name: Q25CUNPDWRK
var.labl: Have an unpaid work to return to

catgry.labl: Temporarily laid off/Reduction in economic activity
Start a new job/business at a definite date in the future

var.name: Q33HAVEJOB
var.labl: Have a job or start a business

var.name: Q33AWHNSTART
var.labl: How soon are you expecting to start work or a business

catgry.labl: In more than 1 month to 3 month
Retired or too old for work

var.name: Q36TIMESEEK
var.labl: How long been trying to find work

var.name: Q38RSNNOTSEEK
var.labl: Reason why not look for work or start business

catgry.labl: Waiting to be recalled to former job
Undergoing training to help find work
No jobs available in the area
Lack of money to pay for transport to look for work
Unable to find work requiring his/her skills
Lost hope of finding any kind of work

var.name: Q311bWHNSTART
var.labl: How soon can you start work or a business

catgry.labl: Later than four weeks from now
Lost job/job ended/laid off/business sold/closed down

Manufacture of gas; distribution of gaseous fuels through mains
COLLECTION, PURIFICATION AND DISTRIBUTION OF WATER
Building of complete constructions or parts thereof; civil engineering
Renting of construction of demolition equipment with operators
Wholesale trade on a fee or contract basis
Wholesale trade in agricultural raw materials, livestock, food, beverages and tobacco
Wholesale trade in non-agricultural intermediate products, waste and scrap
Wholesale trade in machinery, equipment and supplies
Retail trade in food, beverages and tobacco in specialised stores
Other retail trade in new goods in specialised stores
Retail trade in second-hand goods in stores
Repair of personal and household goods
Maintenance and re-pair of motor vehicles
Sale of motor vehicle parts and accessories
Sale, maintenance and repair of motor cycles and related parts and accessories
Hotels, camping sites and other provision of short stay accommodation
Supporting and auxiliary transport activities; activities of travel agencies
Insurance and pension funding, except compulsory social security
Activities auxiliary to financial intermediation, except insurance and pension funding
Activities auxiliary to insurance and pension funding
Real estate activities with own or leased property
Real estate activities on a fee or contract basis
Renting of other machinery and equipment
Renting of personal and household goods N.E.C.
Maintenance and repair of office, accounting and computing machinery
Research and experimental development on natural sciences and engineering
Research and experimental develop-ment on social sciences and humanities
Legal, accounting, bookkeeping and auditing activities; tax consultancy; market research and public pinion research; business and management consultancy
Architectural, engineering and other technical activities
OTHER COMMUNITY, SOCIAL AND PERSONAL SERVICE ACTIVITIES
Activities of business, employers and professional organisations
Motion picture, radio, television and other entertainment activities
Library, archives, museums and other cultural activities
Working for someone else for pay
An employer (employing one or more employees)
Own account worker (not employing any employees)
Helping without pay in a household business

var.name: Q46PENSION
var.labl: Contribution to pension or retirement fund

var.name: Q49MEDICAL
var.labl: Medical aid or health insurance contribution

catgry.labl: Negotiation between myself and employer at company
Bargaining council or other sector bargaining arrangement

var.labl: Hours usually worked - In your second job/business

var.name: Q420OTHERHRSWRK
var.labl: Hours usually worked - In all other jobs/businesses

catgry.labl: Yes, in taking an additional job
Yes, in another job with more hours
Service workers and shop and market sales workers
Plant and machine operators and assemblers
Financial intermediation; insurance; real estate and business services
Service workers and shop and market sales workers
Plant and machine operators and assemblers

var.name: sector1
var.labl: Sector (excludes agriculture from formal and informal sectors)

var.name: Sector2
var.labl: Sector (includes agriculture in the formal and informal sectors)

var.name: at_least_1
var.labl: Involvement in at least one non-market activity

catgry.labl: Involved in at least one activity

var.name: NEET
var.labl: Not in employment, education or training
